mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, reducing recession risk and supporting equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive 20-day forward returns when PAYEMS shows consistent month-over-month gains.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135500 thousand as of Feb 2012 indicates ongoing post-crisis employment recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS growth fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances when PAYEMS month-over-month change exceeds +100k.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.50% and remains in the zero-lower-bound regime, equity risk premiums compress due to suppressed discount rates and reduced opportunity cost of equity relative to cash. This threshold-triggered mechanism activates only when FEDFUNDS crosses below 0.50%, predicting positive 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of Feb 2012 indicates pre-crisis baseline; ZLB regime was active 2008-2015
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 0.50%, 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5% or t-test p > 0.10 vs. zero), or Rank IC < 0.04 in ZLB subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, triggering a negative equity return forecast over 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward. This interaction-driven mechanism requires both series to decline simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 368233M and HOUST at 1795k as of Jan 2012 show post-crisis stabilization
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS and HOUST declines fail to predict negative 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window when both series decline.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 2% annualized signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing momentum, supporting corporate profit margins and equity valuations over a 5-trading-day horizon through near-term earnings surprises.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 113.7 as of Feb 2012 reflects post-2008 recovery in manufacturing
failure_condition: INDPRO growth > 0.15% fails to produce positive 5d returns in > 52% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A regime-conditional mechanism where inflation (CPIAUCSL) acceleration above 2.5% year-over-year in a low-rate environment (FEDFUNDS < 1%) signals policy tightening risk and real rate compression, predicting negative 20-day returns as equity duration risk increases and rate-hike expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 203.7 and FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of Feb 2012
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration in low-rate regime
failure_condition: When both triggers are met, 20d forward returns are not significantly negative (mean return > 0% or t-test p > 0.10 vs. zero), or Rank IC < 0.05 in triggered subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.0% and remains elevated for 3+ consecutive months, signaling labor market deterioration and recession risk, producing negative 60-day equity returns as earnings forecasts are revised downward and equity risk premiums expand.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% as of Feb 2012 indicates sub-5% labor market tightness
regime_hint: labor market deterioration with recession risk
failure_condition: UNRATE > 5.0% sustained for 3+ months fails to predict negative 60d returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.07 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows acceleration above 2.5% annualized, indicating strong aggregate demand and economic momentum that supports equity valuations through higher expected cash flows and lower default risk, predicting positive 60-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11397.6B (2017 dollars) as of Q4 2011 reflects post-crisis recovery trajectory
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth > 2.5% fails to produce positive 60d returns in > 58% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.08 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1,500 thousand units annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously contract, indicating a synchronized demand shock across residential and consumer spending that predicts negative 20-day equity returns through earnings downgrades and increased financial stress.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1500k annualized AND RSAFS month-over-month change < -0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1795k and RSAFS at 368233M as of Jan 2012 show post-crisis stabilization above crisis lows
regime_hint: demand-side shock with housing and consumption weakness
failure_condition: Joint HOUST < 1500k and RSAFS contraction fails to predict negative 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.
